tasks.title: TICKET-001: Add a dependency-free /health liveness endpoint
tasks.prompt:
Implement TICKET-001 from tasks/backlog.md: expose a GET health endpoint that returns HTTP 200 with JSON { "status": "ok", "uptime": <process.uptime() in seconds> }.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT — read before coding:
- The ticket's declared Scope is `src/routes/ (new file only)`, but this repo has no top-level `src/routes/`. The backend is a Fastify modular monolith under `backend/src/`, with feature routes in `backend/src/modules/<name>/<name>.routes.ts` registered in `backend/src/app.ts`. Follow the repo's real convention.
- A `/health` route ALREADY EXISTS inline in `backend/src/app.ts` (~line 184). It is a deep READINESS probe: it pings Prisma (`SELECT 1`) and Redis, and returns 503 `degraded` when either is unavailable. In this sandbox (no DB/Redis/secrets) it returns 503, so it does NOT satisfy the ticket's 'responds 200' acceptance criterion, and it must NOT be broken or removed.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Create a NEW route file `backend/src/modules/health/health.routes.ts` exporting a Fastify plugin that registers a dependency-free LIVENESS endpoint at `GET /healthz` (do NOT reuse `/health` — it is already taken by the readiness probe; `/healthz` is the conventional liveness path and avoids a route collision). Handler returns `reply.status(200).send({ status: 'ok', uptime: Math.floor(process.uptime()) })`. No Prisma, Redis, or queue calls — it must always return 200. Match the existing code style (ESM `.js` import specifiers, TypeScript, async handler).
2. Register it in `backend/src/app.ts` alongside the other `app.register(...)` calls (route registration is explicitly permitted by the ticket). Register at root (no `/api/v1` prefix) so it sits next to the existing `/health`.
3. Add a test `backend/tests/health.test.ts` following the existing test patterns in `backend/tests/` (Vitest + the app builder / Supertest helpers used by e.g. `auth.test.ts`; check `backend/tests/helpers.ts`). Assert GET `/healthz` returns 200 and the body contains `status: 'ok'` and a numeric `uptime` field. Keep it dependency-free so it passes without backend secrets.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT modify any existing file except `backend/src/app.ts` (for route registration) and the two new files.
- Do NOT touch Prisma schema/migrations, .env, CI config, scripts/devsync/, or .claude/.
- Do NOT alter the existing `/health` readiness handler.
- If `contracts/openapi.json` regeneration is expected for new routes, note it in the PR body but only run `cd backend && npm run contract:generate` if the environment supports it; otherwise state it was skipped.
- Run `cd backend && npm run test:run` if possible. If env vars are missing (sandbox), note 'tests not run: sandbox env' in the PR body and proceed.

PR BODY must include: what changed, why (ticket asks for a 200 liveness /health but the existing /health is a DB/Redis readiness probe returning 503 when deps are down, so a dependency-free `/healthz` liveness endpoint was added to satisfy the ticket without breaking readiness semantics), files touched, whether tests ran, and no dependency on other PRs.
